Consider the following statements:
S1 : If the determinant of the matrix is zero, then vectors are linearly independent.
S2 : If a square matrix is orthogonal, then determinant value is ± 1.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• If the determinant of the matrix is zero, then vectors are linearly dependent. So, S1 is incorrect.
• If a square matrix is orthogonal, then determinant value either + 1 or –1. So, S2 is correct.

Q24 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Dense layers do not involve weight parameters.
S2 : Each neuron in a dense layer connected to all neurons in the previous layer.
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Dense layer involve weight parameters. Each connection between neurons in the dense layer has an associated weight that is learned during training. So, S1 is incorrect.
• In a dense layer (also known as a fully connected layer) every neuron is connected to every neuron in the previous layer. So, S2 is correct.

Q29 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : The minimax algorithm explores the complete game tree using depth-first search
S2 : The minimax algorithm explores the complete game tree using best-first search.
Which of the following is true?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The minimax algorithm uses depth-first search (DFS) to explore the complete game tree, aiming to evaluate the best possible moves for both players by going down each branch of the game tree to the terminal nodes. So, S1 is correct.
• The minimax algorithm does not use best-first search. Best-first search is commonly used in algorithms like A* but is not a characteristic of the minimax algorithm, which systematically explores all moves to find the minimax value. So, S2 is incorrect.

Q32 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : LDA (Linear Discriminant Analysis) can not handle multi-collinearity in the data.
S2 : Cross-validation can only identify overfitting but cannot help with underfitting.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• LDA can handle multi-collinearity to some extent, but extreme multi-collinearity may affect its performance. So, S1 is incorrect.
• Cross-validation helps identify both overfitting (if validation error is high) and underfitting (if both training and validation errors are high). So, S2 is incorrect.

Q42 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• Uniform Cost Search (UCS) expands the node with the lowest total cost from the start node to the current node. So, option (a) is correct.
• The branching factor in Iterative Deepening A* (IDA*) is the average number of successors each node has, which influences the search tree's width at each level. So, option (b) is correct
• Alpha-beta pruning skips evaluating branches of the search tree that cannot influence the final decision, improving the Min-Max search's efficiency. So, option (c) is correct
• A* typically requires more space than IDDFS because it stores both the open and closed lists, while IDDFS only needs to store nodes at the current depth. So, option (d) is incorrect

Q54 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Binning reduces dimensionality by aggregating data points into fewer categories.
S2 : OLTP focuses on complex queries and aggregations typical in data warehouses.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Binning does reduce dimensionality by grouping data points into fewer categories, often simplifying analysis. So, S1 is correct.
• OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) focuses on handling short, transactional queries in real-time, such as inserting, updating, and deleting data, rather than complex queries or aggregations typically found in data warehouses, which are more associated with OLAP (Online Analytical Processing). So, S2 is incorrect.

Q57 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(a) Decision trees do require data normalization or scaling.
(b) Information Gain quantifies the reduction in entropy or Gini impurity after splitting a dataset by an attribute.
(c) The curse of dimensionality makes KNN less effective as the number of features increases.
(d) K-Medoids selects actual data points as medoids, enhancing interpretability.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• Decision trees are not sensitive to the scale of data, so normalization or scaling is typically not required. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• Information Gain measures the reduction in entropy or Gini impurity after a split, which helps in deciding the best attribute for splitting. So, option (b) is correct.
• The curse of dimensionality affects KNN as the number of features increases, reducing its effectiveness due to sparse data in high-dimensional spaces. So, option (c) is correct.
• K-Medoids selects actual data points as cluster centers (medoids), which makes the results more interpretable than methods that use calculated centroids. So, option (d) is correct.

Q62 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Drill-down breaks down data into finer levels of detail, enabling deeper analysis.
S2 : Min-max normalization scales data without changing its distribution shape.
Which of the following is correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Drill-down is a data analysis technique that involves breaking down data into finer levels of detail, enabling more in-depth insights. So, S2 is correct.
• Min-max normalization scales data to a fixed range, typically [0, 1], which alters the data’s distribution shape, especially when there are outliers. So, S2 is incorrect.
